//: Phase 1 (MVP) only. Follow-ups from the spec (the tailored /admin console; scheduled cleanup jobs) are separate tracker issues, NOT tasks here. EXECUTE is gated on cloud registration opening (human decision) — see the timing gate in 02-specification.md.

tasks.phaseName: P1 — MVP: admin plugin + self-serve delete/export + audit
tasks.subject: [cloud-account-management] [P1] Admin + audit schema: user/session columns, audit_log table, regenerated migration, isolation test
tasks.description:
Add the admin plugin's columns and the audit table to the cloud (apps/site) schema.

auth-schema.ts: add to `user` — role (text, default 'user'), banned (boolean, nullable), banReason (text, nullable), banExpires (timestamp, nullable); add to `session` — impersonatedBy (text, nullable). Keep the hand-owned regenerate workflow: temporarily add @better-auth/cli, run `better-auth generate` against src/lib/auth.ts (with admin() already registered — see 1.2 ordering note), reconcile the emitted columns (READ the actual snake_case DB names; do not hand-assert ban_reason/ban_expires/impersonated_by), then REMOVE the CLI dep.

New apps/site/src/db/audit-schema.ts: append-only `audit_log` (bigserial id; actor_user_id text notNull; action text notNull; target_user_id text nullable; reason text nullable; metadata text nullable; created_at timestamptz notNull defaultNow; index on (target_user_id, created_at desc)). NO foreign key to `user` (the audit trail must outlive a hard-deleted account) — document why in the file TSDoc. Uphold the telemetry-isolation contract: no FK/join/shared id to marketplaceInstallEvents.

Extend apps/site/src/db/__tests__/schema.test.ts: add audit_log to the allowed account-cluster set and assert it has zero telemetry crossings.

Run `pnpm --filter @dorkos/site db:generate` → one new drizzle/000N_*.sql. Acceptance: typecheck + schema test green; migration file committed; a Neon branch is used to validate the ALTER TABLEs before any prod apply (documented in the PR).
tasks.activeForm: Adding admin + audit schema and migration

tasks.phaseName: P1 — MVP: admin plugin + self-serve delete/export + audit
tasks.subject: [cloud-account-management] [P1] Register admin() + accountLinking + user.deleteUser config + admin-action audit hook in auth.ts
tasks.description:
In apps/site/src/lib/auth.ts createAuth():

1) Register admin() in the plugins array (alongside deviceAuthorization/apiKey/instanceRegistry so one `generate` covers all tables): adminRoles ['admin'], adminUserIds from env.ADMIN_USER_IDS, impersonationSessionDuration 3600, defaultBanReason. Do NOT add defaultBanExpiresIn unless the 1.6.23 type file confirms it exists.
2) account.accountLinking: enabled true, trustedProviders ['google','github','email-password'], allowDifferentEmails false (decision D-A). Verify the literal `enabled`/`allowDifferentEmails` names against the 1.6.23 type file.
3) user.deleteUser: enabled true; sendDeleteAccountVerification → new mailer fn sendDeleteAccountVerification; beforeDelete/afterDelete → recordAudit (decision D-B). Verify the delete verification redirect option identifier before naming it.
4) Admin-action audit hook: a hooks.after middleware keyed on /admin/* paths (mirror the existing /device/token after-hook) that writes an audit_log row per mutating admin op (actor = admin session user, action from path, target from body.userId, reason). Impersonation is audited explicitly.

Acceptance: auth integration tests still pass; admin ops produce audit rows (asserted in 1.8).
tasks.activeForm: Registering admin plugin and auth config

tasks.phaseName: P1 — MVP: admin plugin + self-serve delete/export + audit
tasks.subject: [cloud-account-management] [P1] audit-service.ts — recordAudit + listAudit through the adapter
tasks.description: New apps/site/src/lib/audit-service.ts, mirroring instance-service.ts's adapter-only discipline (auth.$context.adapter or the Drizzle client). recordAudit({ actorUserId, action, targetUserId?, reason?, metadata? }) appends one audit_log row; NEVER logs a secret. listAudit({ targetUserId?, limit, offset }) reads rows newest-first for the export + future console. Acceptance: unit tests assert the right row is written and that no secret field is ever persisted.

tasks.phaseName: P1 — MVP: admin plugin + self-serve delete/export + audit
tasks.subject: [cloud-account-management] [P1] account-service.ts — GDPR data export (secrets stripped)
tasks.description: New apps/site/src/lib/account-service.ts: exportAccountData(auth, userId) assembles the signed-in user's OWN rows into a portability JSON — profile (user), linked providers (account rows with password/accessToken/refreshToken/idToken STRIPPED), API-key + instance metadata (key values stripped), and audit entries about them (via listAudit). Read-only; secrets never included. Add the serving route/server action under apps/site that requires the caller's session and returns their own data only. Acceptance: unit test proves included user rows and EXCLUDED secrets, even as new fields are added.

tasks.phaseName: P1 — MVP: admin plugin + self-serve delete/export + audit
tasks.subject: [cloud-account-management] [P1] Revoke a banned user's API keys on ban (close the heartbeat gap)
tasks.description: banUser revokes SESSIONS but not API keys (verified), so a banned account's linked instances keep authenticating heartbeats. Add a ban hook (or wrap the ban op) that also disables/deletes the user's apikey rows so a banned account cannot keep an instance alive; stamp the corresponding instance.revokedAt for consistency with revokeInstance semantics. Audit the ban+key-revoke as one action. Acceptance: an integration test bans a user with a linked instance and asserts the next heartbeat 401s.

tasks.phaseName: P1 — MVP: admin plugin + self-serve delete/export + audit
tasks.subject: [cloud-account-management] [P1] Client: adminClient() + /account Danger Zone (export + delete)
tasks.description: auth-client.ts: add adminClient() from better-auth/client/plugins. /account UI (apps/site/src/app/(account)/account/... + a features/account slice): a Danger Zone card with 'Export my data' (downloads the portability JSON from 1.4) and 'Delete my account' (double-confirm → authClient.deleteUser() → 'check your email' → erasure on the verification callback). Plain, honest copy; no dark patterns; deletion as easy as the docs allow. Acceptance: component tests for the two flows (export triggers download; delete requires confirm + shows the email step).
tasks.activeForm: Wiring the client admin + account UI

tasks.phaseName: P1 — MVP: admin plugin + self-serve delete/export + audit
tasks.subject: [cloud-account-management] [P1] Break-glass: ADMIN_USER_IDS env + documented founder-promote
tasks.description: Add ADMIN_USER_IDS to apps/site/src/env.ts (comma-separated → string[], Zod-validated, default []), consumed by admin({ adminUserIds }) in 1.2 — the zero-state bootstrap so the founder can operate the moment the plugin ships. Document the one-time durable promote (raw SQL `UPDATE user SET role='admin' WHERE id=...` via the Neon SQL editor) as the sanctioned break-glass step in the docs (1.9). Acceptance: env parse unit test; docs note present.

tasks.phaseName: P1 — MVP: admin plugin + self-serve delete/export + audit
tasks.subject: [cloud-account-management] [P1] Tests: admin ops, self-serve delete cascade, accountLinking, isolation
tasks.description: Integration tests over the in-memory adapter with the mailer mocked (as auth.integration.test.ts does). Cover: promote via adminUserIds; banUser blocks sign-in + revokes sessions (+ API keys per 1.5); unbanUser restores; impersonateUser mints a capped session, writes session.impersonatedBy + an audit row; setRole/setUserPassword/removeUser behave and audit; self-serve deleteUser requires the email token then cascade-erases session/account/apikey/instance AND the afterDelete audit row survives (no FK cascade); accountLinking (D-A): email/password user + same-verified-email Google → one user, two account rows. Plus the schema-isolation extension from 1.1. Each test carries a purpose comment; include failing-capable edge cases. Acceptance: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/site test` green.

tasks.phaseName: P1 — MVP: admin plugin + self-serve delete/export + audit
tasks.subject: [cloud-account-management] [P1] Docs + ADR extraction (authentication.md, configuration.md, /adr:from-spec)
tasks.description: Extend contributing/authentication.md with a 'Cloud account management (P3)' section (admin plugin + role model + break-glass promote; self-serve delete/export; audit-log model; accountLinking policy) and update its Key Files table (admin plugin, audit-schema.ts, audit-service.ts, account-service.ts). Update contributing/configuration.md / env reference for ADMIN_USER_IDS + any new mailer env. Add a runbook note: Neon-branch-before-destructive-ops + the founder-promote step. Run `/adr:from-spec cloud-account-management` to extract the two ADRs the spec names (admin-plugin+audit-log adoption; accountLinking policy) — at EXECUTE time, no draft state. Acceptance: docs:coverage / drift checks pass; ADRs land in decisions/ + manifest.
